jd_text: Sr. Program Manager, Translational Science needed! Please do not apply if you are currently not a US citizen. Also, looking for candidates that live on the east coast of the United States preferred. Summary: This position will report to the Director, Translational Science. The successful candidate will be a hands-on program manager who will contribute to program discussions and decisions while interacting collaboratively with team members to ensure alignment on project action items and deliverables. This role requires driving towards project milestones without sacrificing quality and effective interpersonal communication to keep momentum with internal and external teams. Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions of this position. This list contains the major duties and requirements of the job and is not all-inclusive. Incumbent(s) may be expected to perform role-related duties other than those contained in this document. Design and implement translational strategies for the efficient advancement of clinical programs from phase 1 through pivotal clinical trials. Collaborate with Development Program Leaders to integrate, document, and communicate Translational plans, track progress, and re-forecast as necessary in conjunction with the team Maintain close working relationships with translational team members and functional departments (e.g., Research, Nonclinical, CMC, Clinical, Regulatory, Quality, Program Management) to ensure that all critical parties are aware of translational activity, issues, and contingencies Demonstrate a strong appreciation for research with a focus on translational science and product development Track key milestones, deliverables, risks and dependencies, flag potential delays, and drive mitigation plans. Organize team meetings including agendas, pre-work, minutes, action items and overall translational team correspondence including management of timelines, deliverables, metrics, and budget Develop and implement communication tools that enhance information exchange and stimulate creative project interactions Work in concert with Program Leaders in preparing slide decks and information for ongoing project team meetings, reviews, board meetings, scientific presentations, and other appropriate meetings that involve progression through the key decision points of a program Be a champion of change, propose solutions to problems, support company decisions, and help teams to understand factors and priorities leading to decisions. Qualifications: The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required to perform this job successfully. Required: Ph.D. or Master’s degree in a scientific field with demonstrated business acumen and 7+ years’ experience or equivalent combination of education and experience 4+ years’ experience in translational project leadership or program management in the biotechnology/pharmaceutical industry Knowledge of the drug development process, particularly early-stage clinical trials; strong understanding of technical, business, and regulatory issues Strong knowledge of MS Office and MS Teams and experience working with program/project management tools, including Smartsheet Experience managing external vendors and cross-functional teams. Excellent verbal and written communication, negotiation, team building, presentation, and interpersonal skills Established ability to work independently, prioritize and manage multiple tasks simultaneously, and demonstrated ability to identify and resolve issues Strong initiative and a can-do attitude, excellent organizational skills, proven effectiveness in a quick-changing, fast-paced environment, excellent people skills, and a proven record as a team player Must reside in the United States, preferably on the east coast Preferred: PMP certification Travel Requirements: The position may require limited travel (5-10%).
